sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check state
seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
for n in seg:
    b = n['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    center = (sum(rows)/len(rows), sum(cols)/len(cols))
    if n['color'] in ['W', 'N', 'c', 'G', 'b', 'g']:
        print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, px={n['pixels']}, center={center}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! After pressing SPACE on the bar:
- id=3: W, 16px, at (29.5, 26.0) - white block grew to 16px!
- id=6: b, 4px, at (29.5, 25.5) - the G square turned back to a blue square!
- id=4: N, 12px, at (29.5, 30.0) - player at (29.5, 30.0)
So pressing SPACE while on the bar picked up the G square! The G square reverted to a blue square (b), and the white block grew to 16px. This means the G square is also pickable!
So the mechanic is:
1. SPACE picks up adjacent blue OR G squares (white grows to 16px)
2. SPACE drops the carried square as G (white shrinks to 4px)
3. When you pick up a new square, the previous G reverts to blue
Now I'm carrying the G square (which was the dropped copy of the left square). Let me think about what to do next.
Maybe the goal is to pick up all 3 squares (including the G one) and stack them? But the white block can only carry one square at a time (16px).
Wait, what if I can carry multiple squares? Let me try picking up another square while carrying one. If the white block grows beyond 16px, that would indicate I can carry multiple.
Let me try: I'm carrying the G square (white=16px at 29.5, 26.0). The c square at (25.5, 33.5) is nearby. Let me go there and try to pick it up.
Actually, wait. The white block is at (29.5, 26.0) and the c square is at (25.5, 33.5). They're not adjacent. Let me move to the c square.
Player at (29.5, 30.0), c square at (25.5, 33.5).
- UP: 29.5 -> 25.5 (1 move)
- RIGHT: 30.0 -> 34.0 (1 move)
Then SPACE to try picking up.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content: World model: Picked up G square! White=16px. G squares are also pickable. Let me try picking up another square while carrying one to see if I can stack them.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try to pick up c square at (25.5, 33.5) while carrying G
# Player at (29.5, 30.0)
action(['UP', 'RIGHT', 'SPACE'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
